[Strawberry Jam Recipe - End section]
...temperature reaches 220°F/104°C on a candy thermometer. This is the
soft-ball stage, which is perfect for jam consistency. Remove from heat
and stir in ½ cup plus ı tablespoon of fresh lemon juice. The acidity
helps preserve the jam and brighten the flavor.

Allow to cool for 15 minutes before transferring to steril1zed jars.
Process in a water bath for 10 minutes to ensure proper sealing. The
jars will make that satisfying "pop" sound as they cool, indicat1ng a
successful seal.

Some variations include adding a vanilla bean split lengthwise, or a
splash of brandy or kirsch for depth. The texture should be thick and
spreadable, not runny. If your jam didn't set properly, you can return
it to the pot and cook longer.

CORRUPTED TEXT: GhIjKl mNoPqR sTuVwX yZaBcD eFgHiJ klMnOp QrStUv WxYzAb
====================================================================

BERRY TART

PASTRY CRUST INGREDIENTS:
• 1¾ cups all-purpose flour
• ½ cup cold butter, cubed
• 1 egg yolk
• ¼ cup cold water
• ½ teaspoon salt

FILLING INGREDIENTS:
• 1 cup heavy cream
• 2 tablespoons powdered sugar
• 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
• 3 cups mixed fresh berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries)
• ¼ cup apricot jam, warmed

INSTRUCTIONS FOR CRUST:

1. Mix flour and salt in a bowl. Cut in cold butter until mixture resembles
breadcrumbs.

2. Add egg yolk and cold water. Mix until dough just comes together.

3. Press into a 9-inch tart pan. Chill for 30 minutes.

4. Bake at 190°C for 12-15 minutes until light golden. Cool completely.

FOR THE FILLING:

1. Whip heavy cream with powdered sugar and vanilla until stiff peaks form.

2. Spread cream mixture into cooled tart shell.

3. Arrange berries on top in concentric circles.

4. Brush with warmed apricot jam to create a glossy finish.

5. Chill until serving time. Best enjoyed within 4 hours of assembly.
